regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST: this is a healthy but EXTENDED bull near all-time highs (S&P closed +0.81% at ~7,544 on 7/9) — SPY bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.78% off its 52-week high, +8.84% over a rising 200-day, with 126/252-day momentum +9.2% / +22.5%. The composite regime SCORE is 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable this run (VIX-only), NOT because the tape is neutral — the STRUCTURAL read is clearly bull. WHAT THE PRE-MARKET (7/10 07:30 ET) ADDS on top of an UNCHANGED structural frame: (1) TODAY is NOT catalyst-light — UMich PRELIM July sentiment prints 10:00am ET (inflation-expectations sub-indices the key piece), the first minor test into next week's CPI; (2) index futures are MIXED (S&P ~flat, Nasdaq-100 −0.19%) with SEMIS soft pre-open (MU/INTC/NVDA down) and META firm — a one-session internal tilt, not a signal; (3) VIX ticked UP to ~15.94, so complacency is PAUSING, not deepening; (4) the 7/9 30y auction is CONFIRMED solid (b/c 2.44, indirects 77.74%) — immediate long-end risk is off the table; (5) WTI faded further to ~$72 = an even softer CPI tail; (6) DAL BEAT pre-open = a constructive earnings-season kickoff. Two things still sit on top of an unchanged frame: a FRESH, shallow leadership rotation (financials/defensives lead on 1-month RS but their 3m/6m RS are negative — tech remains the multi-month RS leader), and the one genuine structural crack, MSFT (−28.6% off its high, −12.9% below its 200-day) — improved on a multi-read basis but still bear-stacked below both MAs and so far IDIOSYNCRATIC (QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs). This all sits under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/28-29 carried ~78-85% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts): UMich 7/10 → CPI 7/14 → PPI 7/15 → retail 7/16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29. PRE-MARKET posture: STAY LONG the structural bull through a mixed/semis-soft open — don't chase semis down or rotate out of tech on a one-session wobble; carry a defined-risk hedge into the CPI cluster (UMich 10am the first minor test); do not chase the 1-month rotation; and do not mistake MSFT's improvement for a bottom until it reclaims the 50-day (~404).

backdrop.pricedIn: The tape prices a BENIGN path: a cool/in-line June CPI (7/14) with the oil tail fading, an orderly OPEX pin, and a HOLD at the 7/29 FOMC (carried ~78-85% priced) — but the curve ALSO carries a ~15-18% HIKE tail and NO 2026 cuts, so 'benign' means 'no upside inflation surprise,' not 'easing coming.' Positioning is COMPLACENT — VIX ~15.9 (a 6-read decline now pausing), SPY −0.78% off its all-time high. The 7/9 clean 30y auction removed the immediate long-end risk; the near tests are UMich 7/10 then CPI 7/14. The bar reality must clear: no hot inflation surprise that re-arms the hike tail.
backdrop.asymmetry: Near all-time highs, complacent (low VIX ~15.9, its decline just pausing), extended above trend, into the densest event window of the month (UMich 7/10 → CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29) under a hawkish-hold Fed carrying a live hike tail — risk/reward skews to a DOWNSIDE catalyst against a market carrying almost no fear premium. Protection is cheap; the crowd is offside SHORT vol into KNOWN catalysts. The one genuinely cheap structural asset is the thing left for dead — MSFT (−28.6% off its high) — improving on a multi-read basis, but only a 50-day reclaim (~404) confirms mean-reversion over a dead-cat bounce.
whatWouldChangeIt: SPY losing its 50-day (~739, only −1.7% away) = the first structural crack in the bull, not just an intraday stall

MSFT's weakness SPREADING to the AI-semis, OR the pre-market semis dip persisting into SOXX losing its 50-day (QQQ/VGT breaking their bull-stacks) = idiosyncratic → systematic, the real topping tell — OR, conversely, MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~404) = the de-rate is repairing

a HOT inflation surprise (UMich inflation-expectations 7/10 or CPI 7/14) that re-arms the hike tail, re-fires the long-duration de-rate and breaks the OPEX level

recommendations.rationale: The market is near ATH, complacent (VIX ~15.9, term structure in contango) and EXTENDED above trend, into the densest event window of the month (UMich 7/10 → CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29) — under a hawkish-hold Fed pricing NO 2026 cuts and a ~15-18% HIKE tail, so a hot inflation print has more bite. That is exactly when cheap, defined-risk downside protection is worth carrying. The 7/9 clean auction cleared the immediate long-end risk, so carry the hedge INTO 7/14, not reactively after. SPY flip ~740 (STALE) is the reference — re-pull first. Estimate, not advice.
recommendations.structuralAnchor: Near ATH + complacent (VIX ~15.9) + extended + a dense catalyst cluster under a hawkish-hold Fed — the asymmetry in the backdrop.
recommendations.trigger: add/roll a defined-risk hedge NOW while VIX <16 and the term structure is in contango, ahead of UMich 7/10 and CPI 7/14.
recommendations.invalidation: VIX term structure inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0) — protection is already repricing, the cheap window has closed.

recommendations.subject: Play the FRESH financials/defensives rotation TACTICALLY (XLF, XLV firming) — size it as a 1-month swing, not a structural bet; its 3m/6m RS are still negative
recommendations.rationale: XLF Leads on 1-month RS (+5.29) and is bull-stacked, with the Q2 big-bank kickoff 7/14 as the catalyst — the cleanest tactical rotation long; XLV firming (1m RS 4.46→4.75) as the fresh defensive add. But their 3-month (XLF −2.63) and 6-month (−9.91) RS are negative, so this is a fresh ~1-month move, NOT a structural leadership handoff (tech remains the multi-month RS leader). Trade it bounded. Estimate from RRG + momentum-Δ, not advice.

recommendations.trigger: XLF 1-month RS holding >5 through the first bank reports; the rotation turns STRUCTURAL only if the Leading cohort's 3-month RS crosses >0.
recommendations.invalidation: XLF back under +3 (curve bull-flattens / yields collapse) or 1-month RS rolling back under the benchmark = head-fake.

recommendations.subject: MSFT improved multi-read but is still a STRUCTURAL DOWNTREND — do NOT chase the counter-trend pop; wait for a 50-day reclaim (~404) before treating it as a mean-reversion long

It improved into the 7/9 close (mom5 −0.91→+0.02, mom21 −7.52→−6.65) — a multi-read tactical improvement — but it is still below both MAs, so first overhead is the 50-day (~404), which is RESISTANCE. It is the AI-capex 'show-me' de-rate at the name level and a two-sided EVENT trade into the 7/29 FOMC-day print, not a momentum long yet. Implied move ~6% STALE (7/6). Estimate, not advice.
recommendations.structuralAnchor: MSFT bear-stack; −4.9% below the 50-day, −12.9% below the 200-day; mom252 −22.0%.

It is tactically the weakest sector as crude fades (WTI web-verified ~$72.1 pre-market, down from ~$74.6, on China-demand + OPEC+ overhang) — though structurally not broken (6-month RS +12.53, price +7.75% over its 200-day). recommendations.subject: Do NOT rotate out of tech on the XLK 1-month-RS wobble — it FAILS the persistence gate (shallow, choppy drift) and tech is the structural RS leader
recommendations.rationale: XLK 1-month RS drifted −0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82→−1.2 across the last five snapshots — a shallow, choppy wander in a narrow band, no clean persistent trend, so the persistence gate (≥2-3 same-direction reads, or a structural-level break, or corroboration) is not cleanly met; the one-session pre-market semis dip (MU/INTC/NVDA soft) doesn't meet it either. Structurally XLK is bull-stacked, +21.8% over its 200-day, with the strongest 3-/6-month RS on the board, and SOXX is +52% over its 200-day. This is a do-nothing / hold-tech call. Estimate, not advice.
recommendations.structuralAnchor: XLK bull-stack, +21.8% over the 200-day, 3-month RS +19.49 — the structural frame is intact.

recommendations.subject: Leadership breadth: watch whether the fresh non-tech leadership BROADENS (rising 3-month RS) or fades — that decides durable rotation vs 1-month head-fake

If their 3-month RS climbs toward >0, the leadership handoff is durable and you lean further into cyclicals/defensives; if their 1-month RS rolls back under the benchmark while tech's structural RS holds, it was a 1-month head-fake and tech re-asserts. Estimate from RRG trajectory, not advice.

psychology.read: REGIME (named): a calm, positive-gamma, COMPLACENT risk-on bull near all-time highs (S&P closed +0.81% at ~7,544 on 7/9) — SPY bull-stacked, −0.78% off its 52-week high, +8.84% over a rising 200-day, VIX ~15.9 (the 6-read decline 16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04→15.84 now PAUSING just off its trough — pre-market 7/10 ~15.94). This is STRUCTURALLY a bull; the composite score reads 0 only because Fear&Greed was unfetchable this run (VIX-only). WHAT THE PRE-MARKET (7/10 07:30 ET) ADDS: (1) TODAY is NOT catalyst-light after all — Univ. of Michigan PRELIM sentiment 10:00am ET (inflation-expectations sub-indices the key piece), the first, minor test into next week's CPI cluster; (2) futures are MIXED/mildly-soft (S&P ~flat, Nasdaq-100 −0.19%) with SEMIS leading down pre-open (MU −2.6%, INTC −3.3%, NVDA −0.5%) and META firm — a small semis-down internal rotation to watch at the open, not yet a signal; (3) VIX ticked UP to ~15.94, so complacency is pausing, not deepening; (4) the 7/9 30y auction is CONFIRMED solid (b/c 2.44, indirects 77.74%) — long-end risk off; (5) WTI faded further to ~$72 = an even softer CPI tail; (6) DAL BEAT pre-open = a constructive Q2 earnings-season kickoff. None of this changes the frame. Two features sit ON TOP of an unchanged bull: (1) a FRESH, still-shallow leadership rotation — financials/defensives (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) Lead on 1-month RS but their 3-/6-month RS are NEGATIVE, so tech (XLK 3m RS +19.5) remains the structural RS leader (the XLK 1m-RS drift −0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82→−1.2 is a shallow, choppy wander that fails the persistence gate, as does the one-session semis pre-market dip against a SOXX that is +52% over its 200-day); and (2) one genuine structural CRACK — MSFT bear-stacked, −28.6% off its high and −12.9% below its 200-day, the AI-capex 'show-me' (software) de-rate, improved on a multi-read basis but still below both MAs and so far IDIOSYNCRATIC (VGT/SPMO bull-stacked near highs). CROWD POSTURE: complacent and short-vol into a KNOWN dense catalyst cluster that STARTS TODAY (UMich 7/10 → CPI/PPI/retail 7/14-16 → OPEX 7/17 → FOMC+MSFT 7/29), under a hawkish-hold Fed (no 2026 cuts, a ~15-18% hike tail) that gives a hot inflation print more bite; chasing the 1-month sector rotation; and only just starting to nibble at MSFT. FALSIFIERS of this read: (1) SPY losing its 50-day (~739) = first structural crack; (2) MSFT's weakness SPREADING to QQQ/VGT, or the semis dip persisting into SOXX losing its 50-day (idiosyncratic → systematic) = the real topping tell — OR MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~404) = the de-rate repairing; (3) VIX inverting to backwardation (IVTS >1.0); (4) a HOT inflation surprise (UMich 7/10 or CPI 7/14) that re-arms the hike tail and breaks the OPEX level. Net pre-market posture: STAY LONG the structural bull through a mixed/semis-soft open; don't chase semis down or rotate out of tech on a one-session wobble; carry a CHEAP hedge into the CPI cluster (UMich 10am is the first minor test); trade the fresh rotation as a bounded swing (not a regime change); and do NOT treat MSFT's bounce as a confirmed bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.

This is the AI-capex 'build-it' hardware side and it is INTACT: the notable moves are a violent INTERNAL rotation (AMAT +19.6% / KLAC +8.9% / LRCX +8.9% on the 21-day but each down 9–14% on the 5-day; MRVL −15.8% giving back part of a monster run it still sits +93% over its 200-day on) — swing-grade churn inside a powerful structural uptrend, not a breakdown. The pre-market 7/10 semis softness (MU −2.6%, INTC −3.3%, NVDA −0.5%) is a one-session dip against this +52%-over-200-day structure — churn, not a signal. The structural counter-tell to the MSFT / mega-cap software de-rate. Verify member levels live.

The AI-semis/tech proxy and the STRUCTURAL counter-tell to MSFT: VGT is near highs while MSFT is −28.6% off its high, so the AI-capex weakness is (so far) name-specific, not sector-wide — the pre-market 7/10 semis dip (MU/INTC/NVDA soft) is a one-session move against a still-intact 'build-it' side. A two-sided EVENT trade into the 7/29 FOMC-day print; implied move ~6% STALE (7/6). Wait for a 50-day reclaim before calling a bottom.

experimental.title: Dead-cat vs repair: counter-trend bounce durability in a structurally-broken mega-cap (idea)
experimental.note: Method: when a bear-stacked name below both its 50- and 200-day prints a MULTI-read momentum improvement (this run: MSFT mom5 −0.91→+0.02, mom21 −7.98→−7.8→−7.52→−6.65 across reads, off-high −29.3%→−28.6%), classify it as a nascent bounce and base-rate the forward outcome — does it RECLAIM the 50-day (structural repair, tradeable long) or FAIL below it (dead-cat, the downtrend resumes)? Condition the base rate on (a) whether the name's own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (a supportive backdrop — this run VGT/semis are, even through a one-session pre-market semis dip) and (b) distance to the 50-day (this run ~404, −4.9% overhead). Turns 'MSFT bounced' from a headline into a falsifiable, level-anchored call (reclaim vs reject). Inputs: brief-history.jsonl per-name mom5/mom21 runs + Tier-A maStack/ma50Dist/pctFrom52wHigh + the sector ETF's structure (all have). Directly frames this run's 'do not call the MSFT bottom until it reclaims the 50-day.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Mega-cap structural-breakdown vs index-at-highs divergence detector (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a top-weight name is in a structural DOWNTREND (bear-stack, below its 200-day, deeply off its 52-week high) WHILE the index / its own sector ETF is bull-stacked near highs (this run: MSFT −12.9% below its 200-day, −28.6% off its high, bear-stack; QQQ/VGT bull-stacked near highs). Quantify the divergence (name 200-day distance − ETF 200-day distance, plus the 52-week-range gap) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the name mean-revert UP to the index (idiosyncratic, buy the eventual 50-day reclaim) or does the index roll over TO the name (systematic top, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: the Tier-A structural slice (maStack, ma200Dist, pctFrom52wHigh) — now computed per name + the ETFs. This run it remains the single most important structural read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Fresh-vs-entrenched rotation classifier: 1-month RS leading while 3/6-month RS lag (idea)
experimental.note: Method: classify a sector's leadership as FRESH (1-month RS positive/Leading but 3- and 6-month RS negative — this run: XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU) vs ENTRENCHED (all three horizons positive — this run: none of the tactical leaders; tech is the structural RS leader). Base-rate the forward outcome: does a fresh rotation persist (3-month RS climbs >0 = durable handoff) or fade (1-month RS rolls back = head-fake)? Turns 'rotation' from a one-number snapshot into a horizon-aware, falsifiable call. Inputs: the Tier-A rsMom1m/3m/6m per sector (now emitted). Directly frames this run's 'trade the rotation as a swing, not a regime change.' Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Persistence-gated RS-change detector (noise vs signal) (idea)
experimental.note: Method: for each momentum/RS series, compute the tail consecutive-same-direction run across the last N brief-history snapshots (the rlbrief.js consecutiveRun/isPersistentSignal helpers) and only surface a 'change' when the run is ≥ minRun OR a structural level breaks. This run is a live proof: XLK 1m RS drifted −0.53→−0.94→−0.81→−0.82→−1.2 = a shallow, choppy wander (no clean tail run) = NOISE, so 'tech rotated out' is not actionable, whereas XLF's 4.86→5.89→5.61→5.33→5.29 held a Leading regime = a real swing signal, and VIX's 6-read monotonic decline = a genuine persistent signal. Base-rate the hit-rate of persistence-gated vs raw single-slice signals to quantify how much whipsaw the gate removes. Inputs: brief-history.jsonl (have) + the pure helpers (have, selftest-covered). Promote to a visible 'signal quality' badge if recurring.

experimental.title: Complacency-into-catalyst gauge: persistent VIX decline + event density (idea)
experimental.note: Method: combine a PERSISTENT VIX decline (this run: 16.9→16.74→16.57→16.05→16.04→15.84, a 6-read fall) with a forward event-density count over the next ~10 trading days (this run: CPI/PPI/retail + OPEX + FOMC/MSFT = very dense) and the index's distance from its ATH (−0.78%) to score a 'priced-for-perfection' complacency reading — the setup where cheap protection has the best risk/reward. Base-rate forward drawdown conditioned on high-complacency-into-high-event-density. Inputs: ^VIX series + macroEvents[] + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh (all have). Frames this run's 'carry a cheap hedge into the catalyst cluster' call. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns / realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole calm read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M (add VIX9D/3M to the Tier-A fetch). Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Hawkish-hold hike-tail asymmetry gauge: how a macro print transmits when cut-odds are ~zero (idea)
experimental.note: Method: condition the market's reaction function on the FOMC-priced path, not just the print's surprise. This run the 7/28-29 FOMC (non-SEP) carries ~78-85% HOLD, only ~1-2% cut, and a LIVE ~15-18% HIKE tail (CME FedWatch / Kalshi, carried from prior run — re-verify live), with no 2026 cuts and a hawkish Chair. In that regime a hot CPI does NOT merely delay cuts (there are none to delay) — it RE-ARMS a hike tail, so the downside convexity of an inflation upside-surprise is larger than in a cut-path regime, even at the same surprise magnitude. Build a 'hike-tail bite' score = f(priced hike prob, priced cut prob, distance-to-ATH, VIX complacency) that scales the recommended hedge size ahead of a CPI/PPI. Base-rate forward SPX drawdown on hot-CPI prints split by whether the curve carried a live hike tail vs priced cuts. Inputs: CME FedWatch priced path (web, verify each run) + macroEvents[] + ^VIX + Tier-A pctFrom52wHigh. Directly frames this run's sharper hot-CPI asymmetry. Promote if recurring — no owning tool prices the Fed-path-conditioned reaction function today.
